events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to evaluate pipe bedding & backfill. The scenario: closeout photos show installed work following referenced detail. Clearances, labels, supports, terminations are in place. Inspection card has no open correction note. Reference material: project specs, IPC 306. This is a heavy-industrial site. The stage is rough-complete. The component condition categories: none flagged. So it seems compliant. Decision should be pass. Risk: low (no issues). Work stage: rough-complete as given. Component conditions: empty list. Percent complete: probably high, say 95 or 100? Since it's rough-complete but not yet closed out, maybe 90? But the task says estimate overall progress. It says clearances, labels, supports, terminations in place, inspection card no correction. That suggests work is done correctly, so maybe 100% complete for this element? The stage is rough-complete, meaning installed but not closed out, so maybe 95% complete overall? I'll assign 95. Value: not asked, so 0. Sound source: empty string. Confidence: high, so 0.95. Event time, rate: not needed. Order: not needed. Workable: not asked, so empty list. Findings: none, so empty list. Actions: none, so empty list. Rationale: "Photos and inspection card indicate compliance with project specs and IPC 306; no defects noted." References: maybe "IPC 306" and "project specs". So JSON accordingly.

I'll output the JSON.
